AI.conclusion: The results are highly general and, when specialized to Morrey spaces, weighted Lebesgue spaces, mixed-norm Lebesgue spaces, variable Lebesgue spaces, Orlicz spaces, and Orlicz-slice spaces, yield new findings in each case.

title: Anisotropic 2D FUP and quantum open baker's map
summary: We prove an essential spectral gap for 2D anisotropic quantum open baker's map. This extends the 1D results of Dyatlov--Jin 2017 and the isotropic 2D results of Cohen 2025a. The key ingredient is the anisotropic discrete fractal uncertainty principle (FUP) associated with a 2D anisotropic fractal set called the Bedford--McMullen carpet. We also study the relation between our anisotropic discrete FUP and its continuous counterpart in the spirit of Dyatlov--Jin 2018 and Cohen 2025a. In particular, we prove {continuous FUP} for 2D {anisotropic porous} sets, extending the (high-dimensional) isotropic results of Cohen 2025b. To the best of our knowledge, the anisotropic (line) porosity condition -- a variant of Cohen's line porosity and stronger than ball porosity -- appears to be new to the literature.

AI.tldr: The paper proves an essential spectral gap for 2D anisotropic quantum open baker's maps using a new anisotropic discrete fractal uncertainty principle (FUP) associated with Bedford-McMullen carpets, and extends continuous FUP to 2D anisotropic porous sets with a novel anisotropic line porosity condition.
AI.motivation: Extend prior 1D results (Dyatlov-Jin 2017) and isotropic 2D results (Cohen 2025a) to the anisotropic 2D setting for quantum open baker's maps.
AI.method: Develops an anisotropic discrete fractal uncertainty principle tied to Bedford-McMullen carpets, and introduces a new anisotropic line porosity condition to bridge discrete and continuous FUP.
AI.result: Essential spectral gap for 2D anisotropic quantum open baker's maps; continuous FUP for 2D anisotropic porous sets.
AI.conclusion: The anisotropic line porosity condition is new and stronger than ball porosity, enabling these extensions.

title: A mass-type invariant for smooth metric measure spaces and its relation with the fractional Yamabe problem
summary: On an asymptotically Schwarzschild smooth metric measure space modelled on the Euclidean half-space, we define a mass-type quantity similar to the classical ADM mass in General Relativity and show its geometric invariance properties. Such quantity has a close relation with the fractional Yamabe problem and the relevant Green's function.

AI.tldr: This paper defines a mass-type quantity on asymptotically Schwarzschild smooth metric measure spaces modelled on the Euclidean half-space, shows its geometric invariance properties, and relates it to the fractional Yamabe problem and Green's function.
AI.motivation: To extend the classical ADM mass concept to asymptotically Schwarzschild smooth metric measure spaces on half-space and establish its invariance properties, motivated by connections to the fractional Yamabe problem.
AI.method: The paper defines a mass-type quantity analogous to the ADM mass in General Relativity but adapted to smooth metric measure spaces modelled on the Euclidean half-space, and proves its geometric invariance.
AI.result: A well-defined mass-type quantity with geometric invariance properties is established on asymptotically Schwarzschild smooth metric measure spaces, showing close relation to the fractional Yamabe problem and Green's function.
AI.conclusion: The defined mass-type quantity serves as a meaningful invariant in this geometric setting, linking to fundamental problems in fractional conformal geometry.

title: Spaces with distributional scalar curvature bounded from below: Optimal regularity and positive mass
summary: In this work, we study the positive mass theorem under critical low regularity assumptions using Ricci flow smoothing. We show that asymptotically flat manifolds $(M^n,g)$ of regularity $L^\infty\cap W^{1,n}$ with non-negative distributional scalar curvature have non-negative ADM mass. Furthermore, when the ADM mass vanishes, the manifold is globally isometric to Euclidean space with respect to an integral distance introduced by De~Cecco-Palmieri. This extends the recent work of Hafemann to the critical regularity case. Our approach is based on showing that Riemannian metrics of regularity $L^\infty\cap W^{1,n}$, whose scalar curvature is bounded from below in the distributional sense, admit a Ricci flow smoothing whose scalar curvature is bounded from below by the same initial lower bound in the classical sense. In contrast, Cecchini-Frenck-Zeidler constructed examples of metrics which are in $L^\infty\cap W^{1,p}$ for all $2<p<n$, and whose distributional scalar curvature is bounded from below, that cannot be approximated by smooth metrics with the same scalar curvature lower bound. In this sense, our result is optimal.

AI.tldr: This paper extends the positive mass theorem to critical low regularity ($L^\infty \cap W^{1,n}$) asymptotically flat manifolds using Ricci flow smoothing, proving non-negative ADM mass and rigidity, with optimality supported by counterexamples.
AI.motivation: To prove the positive mass theorem under critical low regularity assumptions, extending prior work by Hafemann to the critical $L^\infty \cap W^{1,n}$ case, where existing counterexamples show the result is optimal.
AI.method: Uses Ricci flow smoothing to show that metrics of regularity $L^\infty \cap W^{1,n}$ with distributionally non-negative scalar curvature can be smoothed while preserving the scalar curvature lower bound in the classical sense.
AI.result: Asymptotically flat manifolds of regularity $L^\infty \cap W^{1,n}$ with non-negative distributional scalar curvature have non-negative ADM mass; zero mass implies global isometry to Euclidean space in the De Cecco-Palmieri integral distance sense.
AI.conclusion: The positive mass theorem holds at the critical regularity $L^\infty \cap W^{1,n}$, and this regularity threshold is optimal as counterexamples exist for all $2<p<n$.

title: Riemannian Positive Mass Theorem in All Dimensions in the Presence of Low-Codimension Singularities
summary: We prove the Riemannian positive mass theorem in all dimensions for asymptotically flat $L^\infty$-metrics with subcritical singular sets. More precisely, we consider complete asymptotically flat manifolds whose metrics are smooth away from a compact singular set of Minkowski dimension less than $n-3+\frac{2}{n}$, and whose scalar curvature is nonnegative on the regular set. We show that the ADM mass of each asymptotically flat end is nonnegative, and that the mass vanishes in some end only in the Euclidean case. For the rigidity statement, we require additionally that the Minkowski dimension of the singular set is not larger than $n-3+\frac{1}{n-1}$. This gives an asymptotically flat analogue of Schoen's codimension-three conjecture for positive scalar curvature. The proof combines a density theorem for singular asymptotically flat metrics, capacity estimates across the singular set, conformal blow-up inspired by Bi-Hao-He-Shi-Zhu [3], and a $μ$-bubble dimension-descent argument adapted from Brendle-Wang [6].

AI.tldr: The paper proves the Riemannian positive mass theorem for asymptotically flat metrics with subcritical singular sets (Minkowski dimension less than n-3+2/n), showing nonnegative ADM mass and rigidity (vanishing mass implies Euclidean) under slightly stronger singularity control (dimension ≤ n-3+1/(n-1)).
AI.motivation: To establish an asymptotically flat analogue of Schoen's codimension-three conjecture for positive scalar curvature, extending the positive mass theorem to metrics with allowable singularities beyond the smooth case.
AI.method: Combines (1) a density theorem for singular asymptotically flat metrics, (2) capacity estimates across the singular set, (3) conformal blow-up inspired by Bi-Hao-He-Shi-Zhu, and (4) a μ-bubble dimension-descent argument adapted from Brendle-Wang.
AI.result: The ADM mass is nonnegative for each asymptotically flat end; mass vanishes in some end only in the Euclidean case. Rigidity requires the singular set's Minkowski dimension ≤ n-3+1/(n-1).
AI.conclusion: The positive mass theorem holds for a broad class of singular asymptotically flat metrics, confirming the expected behavior for subcritical singularities and providing a rigidity result under slightly stronger conditions.

title: Regime-Switching Volterra Operators: Modal Stability and Quenched Amplification
summary: We develop an operator-theoretic framework for finite-dimensional, regime-dependent Volterra equations with completely monotone memory kernels, dissipative network coupling, and Hawkes-type self-excitation. For each fixed regime we construct the associated Volterra resolvent family and prove global well-posedness, continuity across regime switches, and explicit a priori bounds. The main stability result is sharp in the commuting case: after simultaneous diagonalization of the network Laplacian and the excitation operator, each mode obeys a scalar characteristic equation, and global asymptotic stability holds exactly when every modal branching ratio lies below the intensity damping threshold. We also give a norm-based sufficient condition for noncommuting operators and a Perron--Frobenius spectral criterion for nonnegative intensity blocks, showing when norm estimates are conservative. Beyond mean stability, we prove a pathwise finite-range power law for burst amplitudes generated by residence in a Hurwitz but nonnormal regime: under a cone-alignment event, the survival exponent is the ratio of the regime exit rate to a cone-corrected finite-time growth rate bounded above by the logarithmic norm of a fixed Markovian realization in the chosen Euclidean metric. A complementary idealized-feedback result shows how a logarithmic-norm contraction caps the amplification band. Finally, we derive the deterministic intensity block as a mean-field limit of a relaxing long-memory Hawkes system with regimes. Numerical experiments on modal equations, a small-world network, and a switched nonnormal ODE validate the sharp threshold and the finite-range amplification mechanism without using the closed-form tail formula as input.

AI.tldr: This paper develops a mathematical framework for regime-dependent Volterra equations with memory kernels and self-excitation, proving stability conditions and pathwise amplification bounds, validated by numerical experiments.
AI.motivation: To provide rigorous stability and amplification analysis for finite-dimensional Volterra systems with regime-switching, completely monotone memory, and Hawkes-type self-excitation, which arise in networked systems with memory and regime changes.
AI.method: Operator-theoretic framework using Volterra resolvent families, simultaneous diagonalization for commuting cases, Perron-Frobenius spectral criteria for nonnegative intensity blocks, and cone-alignment events for pathwise power-law bounds.
AI.result: Sharp stability condition in the commuting case (modal branching ratio below intensity damping threshold), norm-based sufficient condition for noncommuting case, pathwise finite-range power law for burst amplitudes under cone-alignment, and mean-field limit connecting to relaxing long-memory Hawkes systems.
AI.conclusion: The theoretical results are validated through numerical experiments on modal equations, small-world networks, and switched nonnormal ODEs, confirming the sharp threshold and finite-range amplification mechanism.
